capabilities.name: Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15)
capabilities.description: Apply the five-step ASC 606 / IFRS 15 model — identify contract, performance obligations, transaction price, allocation, and recognition — for businesses selling bundled, subscription, or multi-element arrangements. Critical for SaaS and professional services.

requirements.text: Maintain a contract master data repository that captures contract terms, identified performance obligations, and their characteristics (distinct goods/services, timing, pricing).

Build and maintain a standalone selling price (SSP) library with methods for determining SSP (observable market price, adjusted market assessment, expected cost plus margin) for all distinct performance obligations.
Automatically allocate transaction price to identified performance obligations using SSP-based proportional allocation or other systematic methods.
proxy_refs: Automatic allocation of transaction price across obligations

requirements.text: Classify each performance obligation as satisfied at a point in time or over time, and apply the appropriate recognition method (immediate vs. systematic over the satisfaction period).
proxy_refs: Recognition schedule (point in time vs over time)

requirements.text: Track deferred revenue and contract liabilities, recognizing revenue as performance obligations are satisfied and updating balances for contract modifications.
Handle contract modifications by reassessing performance obligations, transaction price, and allocation, and adjust revenue recognition prospectively or retrospectively as required.

Estimate and constrain variable consideration (discounts, rebates, penalties, contingent fees) using the expected value or most likely amount method, applying the constraint principle to avoid overstatement.
Generate ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure reports including revenue by performance obligation type, timing of recognition, significant judgments, and contract asset/liability balances.
Produce revenue waterfall and backlog reports showing recognized revenue, deferred revenue, and forward-looking contract value by period and customer segment.

features.name: Contract Modifications
features.description: Reassess and adjust revenue recognition for contract changes and variable consideration.

features.name: Reporting & Analytics
features.description: Generate ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosures, waterfall, and backlog reports.

capabilities.name: Data Security, Privacy & Consent
capabilities.description: Finance data is the most sensitive category in any business — PDPA consent, encryption at rest and in transit, retention policies, audit trails, and secure destruction. Universal — PSG requires every pre-approved vendor to meet PDPA compliance, Cyber Essentials Mark, and VA/PT audits regardless of which module they cover.

requirements.text: Capture and maintain audit trail for all PDPA consent records related to personal data processing

requirements.text: Support secure deletion of customer and employee personal data upon authorized request

requirements.text: Encrypt financial data at rest using industry-standard encryption algorithms
